title: aldama radula
intro: Aldama radula, also known as Mexican tea, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. Its health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating sore throats, and acting as a mild laxative, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. The leaves and flowers of the Aldama radula plant can be prepared as a tea by infusing them in hot water, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures for various health benefits.
